title: destrudo stuff Asuka Shinji thread split
display_title: destrudo stuff Asuka Shinji thread split
wikitext:
[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Fri Aug 29, 2008 1:50 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: They do say death instinct . So yes, a death wish does generate destrudo so a death wish=no return. So that means Asuka wanted to be there, by Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Aug 29, 2008 6:12 pm TemporalKnight wrote: Even if the destrudo argument works she didn't have to come back because Shinji was there.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Fri Aug 29, 2008 6:33 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Formless One wrote: That's dictionary.com again.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Aug 29, 2008 6:39 pm Formless One wrote: My point is, if you can only find that info in a dictionary, and the actual psychology sources don't support that information and relevant interpretation, then that information is still in question.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Fri Aug 29, 2008 6:51 pm One of your own sources agrees. What do you have to say to that?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Aug 29, 2008 7:00 pm Formless One wrote: Tell me, which source is that? I don't remember seeing any. I would indeed take your interpretation more seriously if I knew where it came from.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Fri Aug 29, 2008 7:08 pm Good enough?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Aug 29, 2008 7:15 pm Formless One wrote: Death instinct does not, in my mind, mean death wish, although it is closer to the point.

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Fri Aug 29, 2008 9:59 pm Lucretius wrote: Edit: Wait, how did this get moved to the destrudo thread?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Aug 29, 2008 10:05 pm Fixed, carry on.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Aug 30, 2008 3:00 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Tines. Every time that stuff pops up I pull destrudo back out.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Aug 30, 2008 3:20 pm It's as simple as wanting death means you're generating destrudo in imbalanced perportions. Destrudo prevents people from reembodying. Ritsuko made the comment don't you want to come back ? in episode 20 after we have mention of a destrudo reaction. What the LCL sea is has no bearing on what destrudo does or why it does it.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Sat Aug 30, 2008 7:05 pm Now, although wikipedia probably isn't as high up there as some of the psychological terminology sources, it does provide some good information. Particularly that Destrudo is the "energy" that it arises from the Death Drive (also referred sometimes as thanatos) which is more like the "instinct/wish" part that been talked about here (though Freud calls it a "drive" rather than an instinct). He also says this on the Death Drive: "an urge inherent in all organic life to restore an earlier state of things". Now, to me, this is pretty close to what happens when tanging... This means that destrudo is the actual mechanism acting to fulfill this "drive" (of course this is balanced out by the Eros (life drive), and its accompanying energy , libido), not the actual "impulse/instinct/wish". As for source backing, I'd take Freud's own interpretations over any other or current definitions. (i.e. Freudian psychology over non-Freudian psychology). In other words, I think there's been a mix up between destrudo and thanatos. Thanatos is the "instinct", destrudo is simply the "energy" carrying out that "instinct".

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Aug 30, 2008 7:23 pm No, there is no mix up on my part.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 30, 2008 9:01 pm Imagine what a thread like this would be like if we were talking about the Dirac Sea. "First we must get an adequate definition of the Dirac Sea from a reliable external source, THEN we can talk about what it actually means in NGE, even though there may actually be relatively little resemblance between the two." The correlation doesn't need to be 100%, people. This is NGE, not a serious treatise on Freud's psychoanalytical theory. Anno probably just encountered the term "destrudo" while he was doing his post-episode 16 readings, thought it would be "useful", and the rest was madness.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Sat Aug 30, 2008 10:44 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: No, there is no mix up on my part.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sun Aug 31, 2008 1:39 am Reichu wrote: "First we must get an adequate definition of the Dirac Sea from a reliable external source, THEN we can talk about what it actually means in NGE, even though there may actually be relatively little resemblance between the two."

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Mon Sep 01, 2008 5:25 am Zaque wrote: Now, although wikipedia probably isn't as high up there as some of the psychological terminology sources, it does provide some good information. Particularly that Destrudo is the "energy" that it arises from the Death Drive (also referred sometimes as thanatos) which is more like the "instinct/wish" part that been talked about here (though Freud calls it a "drive" rather than an instinct). He also says this on the Death Drive: "an urge inherent in all organic life to restore an earlier state of things". Now, to me, this is pretty close to what happens when tanging... This means that destrudo is the actual mechanism acting to fulfill this "drive" (of course this is balanced out by the Eros (life drive), and its accompanying energy , libido), not the actual "impulse/instinct/wish". As for source backing, I'd take Freud's own interpretations over any other or current definitions. (i.e. Freudian psychology over non-Freudian psychology). In other words, I think there's been a mix up between destrudo and thanatos. Thanatos is the "instinct", destrudo is simply the "energy" carrying out that "instinct".

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Mon Sep 01, 2008 5:25 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: In this case it's quite obvious that Anno is linking Thanatos/Destrudo as the will to return to a previous state - specifically where we're all back in the womb.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 01, 2008 6:07 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Reichu wrote: "First we must get an adequate definition of the Dirac Sea from a reliable external source, THEN we can talk about what it actually means in NGE, even though there may actually be relatively little resemblance between the two."
